subtopic_name: Collaborating with local LLMs (Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp)
summary: Local LLM runtimes (Ollama on port 11434, LM Studio on 1234, llama.cpp's llama-server on 8080, vLLM) all expose OpenAI-compatible REST endpoints, and several now also speak the Anthropic Messages API, which lets a developer pair Claude Code or the Agent SDK with on-device models for a TSLA-style research workflow. Common patterns are (a) Claude as orchestrator delegating bulk summarization, embedding, and overnight iteration to a local model via an MCP server such as OllamaClaude, (b) routing private SEC filings or notes to the local model so they never leave the machine, and (c) cost arbitrage: local Q4 inference is effectively free of marginal token cost while Claude handles high-quality reasoning. A 70B model at Q4_K_M needs roughly 40-46 GB VRAM, an 8B model only ~6 GB, making consumer hardware viable for the worker tier.

key_claims.claim: Ollama exposes OpenAI-compatible /v1/chat/completions, /v1/completions, /v1/embeddings, /v1/models, and /v1/responses endpoints at http://localhost:11434/v1/, with streaming, tool calling, vision, and JSON mode supported.

LM Studio's local server defaults to http://localhost:1234/v1 and implements OpenAI-style /v1/chat/completions, /v1/completions, /v1/embeddings, /v1/models, and /v1/responses (Codex) endpoints with no API-key auth, relying on loopback binding for security.

llama.cpp's llama-server binary provides OpenAI-compatible chat/completions, embeddings, and a separate /v1/messages endpoint that implements the Anthropic Messages API (including tool use via --jinja and SSE streaming), defaulting to port 8080.

Claude Code can be pointed at a local Ollama server by setting ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:11434, ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=ollama, and ANTHROPIC_API_KEY='', because Ollama exposes an Anthropic-compatible API path.

The OllamaClaude MCP server implements an orchestrator-worker pattern in which Claude Code orchestrates and 11 MCP tools (generate_code, review_file, analyze_files, etc.) delegate file-aware work to a local Ollama model, with reported reductions in Anthropic token consumption of up to 98.75% on file-review workloads.

Llama 3.1 8B at Q4_K_M occupies about 4.9 GB on disk and ~6.2 GB VRAM at 8k context, while Llama 3.3 70B at Q4_K_M is about 42.5 GB on disk and ~45.6 GB VRAM, making 8B viable on a single 8 GB consumer GPU and 70B requiring 48 GB-class hardware (e.g., 2x24 GB).

vLLM exposes an OpenAI-compatible server (Chat, Completions, Responses, Embeddings) and is the recommended high-throughput backend for large open models such as DeepSeek-R1 and Qwen variants, though full-precision DeepSeek-R1 requires ~16 H100-class GPUs for production-grade serving.

Hosted open-model APIs (Together, Fireworks, Groq) typically price at $0.05-$0.90 per 1M tokens, versus Claude Sonnet 4.5 at $3 input / $15 output per 1M tokens, so routing 70% of low-stakes summarization to a local or hosted open model and reserving Claude for ~10% of premium reasoning is the dominant cost-optimization pattern.

DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-14B at Q4_K_M needs roughly 9 GB on disk and 11 GB VRAM at 8k context, making a reasoning-capable distilled model practical on a single 12-16 GB consumer card for offline research iteration.

data_points.label: Hosted open-model API price (low end, e.g. DeepSeek/Llama via Together)
Token reduction reported by OllamaClaude MCP file-aware tools
Recommended Claude Code context window for Ollama backend

recommended_charts.title: VRAM required for popular local models at Q4_K_M (8k context)
Per-1M-token price: Claude Sonnet 4.5 vs hosted open-model range

limitations: Pricing data is sourced from a third-party comparison aggregator rather than Anthropic's price page directly, so the Claude Sonnet 4.5 figures should be re-verified against anthropic.com before any cost model is shipped. VRAM numbers are at 8k context; longer contexts (32k-128k), which a research workflow may need, push KV-cache usage substantially higher and were not deeply explored here.
